nodes.label: ICE Methodology (Intent-Context-Expectations)
nodes.rationale: Separates desired outcomes (Intent), system reality (Context), and acceptance criteria (Expectations) to enforce thorough discovery before implementation. Priority order Expectations > Context > Intent ensures acceptance gates override aspirational or contextual guidance.

nodes.label: Human Approval Gate (mandatory proposal approval)
nodes.rationale: Agents must not self-approve proposals. A human must explicitly approve implementation before it begins, preventing autonomous drift from stated intent and preserving human oversight at the most consequential decision point.

nodes.label: Work Item Lifecycle (proposed -> completed)
nodes.rationale: Work files use a sortable YYYYMMDD-NNN naming convention so execution order matches file sort order. Completed proposals remain in work/1_proposed/ with status=completed and a link to the completion report, keeping history visible without duplication.

nodes.label: Template Retention Rule
nodes.rationale: Templates are durable reference artifacts; deleting or renaming them breaks discoverability for future agents. Superseding a template requires adding a new version rather than removing the prior one.

hyperedges.label: ICE Methodology enforced by Expectations, Context, and Intent priority hierarchy
Human approval gate enforced across AGENTS.md, ICE methodology, and proposal lifecycle
